sections.paragraph: an unused root. Arab. ماد Med. Ye; to be moved, agitated, e.g. used of an earthquake (cogn. מוּט, נוּד, נָדַד). The verb itself in Pilel מֶֹודֵד is perhaps found, Hab 3:6, “(God) standeth וַיְמֹודֶד אֶרֶץ and moveth the earth,” compare LXX., Ch. But see under מָדַד Poel.-Besides this, hence is derived the noun תָּמִיד.
